text: I really miss Ames Tap Water. I have just moved to a new city. I drank from the tap. The taste was weird but I think it might be just too cold. So I just followed my old Ames routine and boiled tap water using kettle to make tea. When I poured out the boiled water, the water surface looked a bit different but I do not think much. I told myself I was too busy to pay attention to water when I was in Ames. And later in the day I went back to drink from the kettle and I found lots of white color powder like things in my mug. I checked the kettle and I thought my new roommate never cleaned the kettle so I scrubbed it. And two days later same things happened again. It turns out the kettle will be covered with mineral within two days ffs. I really miss Ames water now. I used to wipe the kettle bottom once in a few months. That's not something I expect to happen😭 I used to think Brita water filter is a joke. Now I am the joke. It seems like I trade the cleanest tap water with moist winter nostrils

text: To the person who took this polaroid.... It was KQ, two years ago. I was headed to the scavenger hunt room and I asked you for directions. My cat, at the time, was 15, 105 in "cat years" You had a Polaroid camera and asked to take a picture, and you gave me the picture when it was done. The cat in this picture, my best friend in the whole world, passed away a year later. I still carry this polaroid in my wallet. I see it every day I don't know your name. text: Incoming PhD student: Chances of getting Freddy Court this late? Should I cancel my contract before the grace period ends?. Hey everyone, I’m an incoming PhD student and I’m in a bit of a housing dilemma right now. I signed my DOR housing contract on May 15, so I missed the March 1 priority deadline. My only two preferences are Frederiksen Court (2 Bed/2 Person Private or 4 Bed/4 Person Private). I emailed DOR, and they said administrative assignments start next Wednesday (May 27) and go through June. The problem is my 14-day cancellation grace period ends on **May 29**, and DOR said they can’t guarantee I’ll actually have my assignment by then. If I don't get my assignment by May 27 or May 28, should I just cancel my contract to be safe? I really don't want to get past the 29th, get stuck with a massive cancellation penalty, and end up placed in a random undergrad dorm I didn't want. * What are the actual chances of getting a private room in Freddy Court when signing mid-May? * If I cancel next week, is it going to be hard to find a decent off-campus apartment in Ames this late? Any advice would be hugely appreciated. Thanks!

If she's so stressed from Statics and studying 20+ hours, maybe she should focus on that instead of Reddit. My roommates saw the comments and are still saying I messed up the relationship, but idk. I'm heading to the Carver second floor bathroom in a bit to blow off some steam after all this. text: Important date in ISU History (Missed it yesterday 😬). From the Ames History Museum FB page: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1DPTYTFhXN/ Today in Ames history: On May 6, 1970, more than 4,000 students and community members marched from campus to downtown Ames to protest the draft, American involvement in Cambodia as part of the Vietnam War, and the shooting at Kent State University. Participants disrupted ROTC drills on campus, sang protest songs at the Armory building, blocked traffic on Lincoln Way, and protested at the Story County Selective Service Board office in downtown Ames. The following day, 24 demonstrators were arrested during a sit-in at the Selective Service office. Local protestors also added an entry called "March for Concern" to that year's VEISHEA parade on May 9, which was followed by another 2,000-person rally on Central Campus. This photo shows protestors blocking traffic at Lincoln Way and Beach Avenue on May 6, 1970. The March for Concern and other local marches are featured in the "Unfinished Revolutions" section of our current exhibit, "We the People of Ames: A USA 250 Exhibit." The museum is open Tue-Sat, 10am-4pm

text: Post-Graduation Job Search Results - B.S. in Mechanical Engineering from ISU. I graduated in May of this year. For anyone coming down the tube at the end of this next semester, this is what the search may look like for you. Some additional notes and stats: * Graduated with 2.98 GPA, one internship, and two years as a student worker for the College of Design Output Center. Tech electives were oriented towards general industry skills (I E 305, TSM 310, CON E 380, M E 280X). No resume-worthy club projects. * Roles applied for were primarily product, design, or manufacturing engineering positions. * I applied for roles in IA, SD, MN, WI, IL, MO, KS, NE, and ND. * I got \*very\* close to receiving offers in June and July, but ultimately didn't find success until this week. * The role I recieved an offer for was in-state, in a generalist ME role (primarily product and manufacturing work), applied for through CyHire. Moral of the story: get internships, do club projects, don't blow off your calc lectures

text: STOP, zoom out, think about it. The jointly observable universe, everything that happens to you and even part of your own body and actions is a canvas that you have no control over. Your actions are a paintbrush. You exist as a delicate balance of your unique window into this universe, consciousness, contained in a limited physical form in a logical and apathetic physical world. Altered by experience and chemistry our brain dictates much of our behavior but expressing no free will is to leave the canvas untouched by the paint brush. The totality of your life is the highest form of art and your actions are the paint brush. How will you paint your universe with your actions? What will your painting look like when it's done? Will you let the discordant canvas dominate the painting or will you strive to create something beautiful on a difficult medium? -The Canvas Poetry motivation

text: i loathe ice. back in february 2014 i slipped on an ice patch outside carver hall and ended up breaking my leg; two years later i slipped on a patch of ice on stange and got a concussion. this has led me to have animosity to ice, and since faculty are allowed to have opinions about the weather, i wanted to share mine. my opinions are my own and do not necessarily reflect the opinions of iowa state university. i have been made particularly anxious by the recent videos that have been coming out of minnesota. too many people have been injured by encounters with ice in the local neighborhoods. and no one, regardless of who they are or where they come from, deserves to be harmed by ice. walking around our city and our campus this past week it has been lovely to not have to deal with ice, and i hope that it stays that way. however, there is a chill in the air, and i cannot predict what will happen in the short term. for the long term i am an optimist, and i believe that warmer days will come where ice will no longer wreak havoc. until those warmer days, please exercise caution if you encounter ice, and remember that we are a community; what happens to any one of us, can happen to all of us. so let's always be ready to give a helping hand when needed. steve butler

text: What’s with the politeness?. I’m new to Iowa. Whenever I go out or bike around, people greet me warmly. I hear the phrases “thank you,” “sorry,” and “have a nice day” about 10 times a day. I work in dining, and I’m regularly addressed as “sir.” I’ve never been called “sir” before, especially from people who look older than me. These are college students, right? People say Canadians are polite, but I’ve been all over Canada and they don’t compare to this amount of hospitality. Don’t get me wrong, there are some people who disrespect me, but they are the tiniest of minorities. I just wanted to share my appreciation of the people in this city and university. Thanks to you, I’m having a blast!
label: substantive

text: Missing orientation for MUSIC 1020 (online). Has anyone taken this class (Intro to Music Listening w/ Tam) before and also missed the orientation? Asking because I've already emailed my professor explaining my situation and she sent me an AI template email back and clearly did not read my email. Just wanted to see if anyone has had this happen before while I wait for her email back (and also shout to the void a little). I would be missing the orientation (only) since I only found out about it on Saturday (and it's on Tuesday at 1pm), and happen to have an important appointment scheduled for the same time (which I already tried and failed to reschedule). I'm so sick of these stupid online classes being listed as "asynchronous" but failing to mention important things like this on Workday. Like this is an ONLINE SUMMER class for MUSIC LISTENING!!!! Why do so many of these professors have ZERO respect for student's time? The entitlement some of them have just blows my fucking mind. I'm honestly considering dropping, but then I'll have to find another online 3-credit humanities course that doesn't sound insufferable, and I should probably do that sooner rather than later. **Update:** Decided to cut my losses and swapped for MUSIC 3040, lets gooo
